main.c(3) Library Functions Manual main.c(3)

NAME

main.c - ascii-chat Unified Binary - Mode Dispatcher and Entry Point

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct mode_descriptor_t
Metadata for a mode.

Macros


#define APP_NAME 'ascii-chat'
#define VERSION ASCII_CHAT_VERSION_FULL
#define REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS()

Typedefs


typedef int(* mode_entry_point_t) (void)

Functions


atomic_t g_should_exit ATOMIC_INIT_AUTO (g_should_exit)
bool should_exit (void)
void signal_exit (void)
void set_interrupt_callback (void(*cb)(void))
void setup_signal_handlers (void)
int main (int argc, char *argv[])

Detailed Description

ascii-chat Unified Binary - Mode Dispatcher and Entry Point

This file implements the main entry point for the unified ascii-chat binary, which provides server, client, mirror, and discovery service functionality in a single executable.

The dispatcher now delegates ALL option parsing (including mode detection and binary-level options) to options_init(), then simply dispatches to the appropriate mode-specific entry point based on the detected mode.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

2025

Version

3.0

Definition in file main.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define APP_NAME 'ascii-chat'

#define REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS()

Value:

do {                                                                                                                 \


  static bool _global_atomics_registered = false;                                                                    \


  if (!_global_atomics_registered) {                                                                                 \


    NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_should_exit, "application_exit_flag", NULL);                                            \


    _global_atomics_registered = true;                                                                               \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)




Definition at line 178 of file main.c.


178 #define REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS()                                                                                      \
179   do {                                                                                                                 \
180     static bool _global_atomics_registered = false;                                                                    \
181     if (!_global_atomics_registered) {                                                                                 \
182       NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_should_exit, "application_exit_flag", NULL);                                            \
183       _global_atomics_registered = true;                                                                               \
184     }                                                                                                                  \
185   } while (0)

atomic_t
  g_should_exit ATOMIC_INIT_AUTO (g_should_exit )


Global flag indicating application should exit (used by all

References g_should_exit.






int
  main (int argc, char * argv[])


Definition at line 442 of file main.c.


442                                  {
443   // Set global argc/argv for early argv inspection (e.g., in terminal.c)
444   g_argc = argc;
445   g_argv = argv;
446   // Validate basic argument structure
447   if (argc < 1 || argv == NULL || argv[0] == NULL) {
448     fprintf(stderr, "Error: Invalid argument vector\n");
449     return 1;
450   }
451 
452   // Show cursor early in case a previous session crashed with it hidden
453   (void)terminal_cursor_show();
454 
455   // Initialize the named registry for debugging (allows --debug-state to show registered synchronization primitives)
456 #ifndef NDEBUG
457   named_init();
458   // Initialize atomic operations debug tracking
459   debug_atomic_init();
460   // Register global atomics with descriptive names for debug sync state monitoring
461   REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS();
462   // Static atomics are now auto-registered via ATOMIC_INIT_AUTO macro at definition time
463   // Register all packet types from the packet_type_t enum
464   named_registry_register_packet_types();
465 #endif
466 
467   // Register the main thread IMMEDIATELY after named_init() to ensure it's available for all subsequent allocations
468 #ifndef NDEBUG
469   NAMED_REGISTER_THREAD(asciichat_thread_self(), "main", NULL);
470   // Also save main thread ID for memory reporting (must be very early)
471   debug_sync_set_main_thread_id();
472 #endif
473 
474   // VERY FIRST: Scan for --color BEFORE ANY logging initialization
475   // This sets global flags that persist through cleanup, enabling --color to force colors
476   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
477     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--color") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "--color=true") == 0) {
478       g_color_flag_passed = true;
479       g_color_flag_value = true;
480       break;
481     } else if (strcmp(argv[i], "--color=false") == 0) {
482       g_color_flag_passed = true;
483       g_color_flag_value = false;
484       break;
485     } else if (strncmp(argv[i], "--color=", 8) == 0) {
486       g_color_flag_passed = true;
487       // Default to true for any other --color=X value
488       g_color_flag_value = true;
489       break;
490     }
491   }
492 
493   // SECOND: Scan for --grep BEFORE ANY logging initialization
494   // This ensures ALL logs (including from shared_init) can be filtered
495   // Supports multiple --grep patterns (ORed together)
496   for (int i = 1; i < argc - 1; i++) {
497     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--grep") == 0) {
498       const char *pattern = argv[i + 1];
499       asciichat_error_t filter_result = grep_init(pattern);
500       if (filter_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
501         fprintf(stderr,
502                 "ERROR: Invalid --grep pattern or invalid flags: \"%s\" - use /pattern/flags format (e.g., "
503                 "\"/query/ig\" or \"/literal/F\")\n",
504                 pattern);
505         return 1;
506       }
507       i++; // Skip the pattern argument
508     }
509   }
510 
511   // Detect terminal capabilities early so colored help output works
512   // Logging will be initialized by asciichat_shared_init() before options_init()
513   log_redetect_terminal_capabilities();
514   terminal_capabilities_t caps = detect_terminal_capabilities();
515   caps = apply_color_mode_override(caps);
516 
517   // Warn if Release build was built from dirty working tree
518 #if ASCII_CHAT_GIT_IS_DIRTY
519   if (strcmp(ASCII_CHAT_BUILD_TYPE, "Release") == 0) {
520     fprintf(stderr, "⚠️  WARNING: This Release build was compiled from a dirty git working tree!\n");
521     fprintf(stderr, "    Git commit: %s (dirty)\n", ASCII_CHAT_GIT_COMMIT_HASH);
522     fprintf(stderr, "    Build date: %s\n", ASCII_CHAT_BUILD_DATE);
523     fprintf(stderr, "    For reproducible builds, commit or stash changes before building.\n\n");
524   }
525 #endif
526 
527   // Load color scheme early (from config files and CLI) before logging initialization
528   // This allows logging to use the correct colors from the start
529   options_colorscheme_init_early(argc, argv);
530 
531   // Parse --color-scheme from argv early to set logging colors for help output
532   const char *colorscheme_name = "pastel"; // default
533   for (int i = 1; i < argc - 1; i++) {
534     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--color-scheme") == 0) {
535       colorscheme_name = argv[i + 1];
536       break;
537     }
538   }
539 
540   // Load and apply colorscheme to logging BEFORE options_init() so help gets colors
541   color_scheme_t scheme;
542   if (colorscheme_load_builtin(colorscheme_name, &scheme) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
543     log_set_color_scheme(&scheme);
544   } else {
545     // Fall back to default pastel if scheme not found
546     if (colorscheme_load_builtin("pastel", &scheme) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
547       log_set_color_scheme(&scheme);
548     }
549   }
550 
551   // Initialize logging colors so they're ready for help output
552   log_init_colors();
553 
554   // EARLY PARSE: Find the mode position (first positional argument)
555   // Binary-level options must appear BEFORE the mode
556   int mode_position = -1;
557   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
558     if (argv[i][0] != '-') {
559       // Found a positional argument - this is the mode or session string
560       mode_position = i;
561       break;
562     }
563 
564     // Skip option and its argument if needed
565     // Check if this is an option that takes a required argument
566     const char *arg = argv[i];
567     const char *opt_name = arg;
568     if (arg[0] == '-') {
569       opt_name = arg + (arg[1] == '-' ? 2 : 1);
570     }
571 
572     // Handle --option=value format
573     if (strchr(opt_name, '=')) {
574       continue; // Value is part of this arg, no need to skip next
575     }
576 
577     // Options that take required arguments
578     if (strcmp(arg, "--log-file") == 0 || strcmp(arg, "-L") == 0 || strcmp(arg, "--log-level") == 0 ||
579         strcmp(arg, "--config") == 0 || strcmp(arg, "--color-scheme") == 0 || strcmp(arg, "--log-template") == 0) {
580       if (i + 1 < argc) {
581         i++; // Skip the argument value
582       }
583     }
584   }
585 
586   // EARLY PARSE: Determine mode from argv to know if this is client-like mode
587   // The first positional argument (after options) is the mode: client, server, mirror, discovery, acds
588   bool is_client_like_mode = false;
589   if (mode_position > 0) {
590     const char *first_arg = argv[mode_position];
591     if (strcmp(first_arg, "client") == 0 || strcmp(first_arg, "mirror") == 0 || strcmp(first_arg, "discovery") == 0) {
592       is_client_like_mode = true;
593     }
594   }
595 
596   // EARLY PARSE: Extract log file from argv (--log-file or -L)
597   // Must appear BEFORE the mode
598   // Generate default log path based on build type and mode
599   char default_log_path[PATH_MAX];
600   asciichat_mode_t detected_mode = MODE_INVALID;
601   if (mode_position > 0) {
602     const char *mode_str = argv[mode_position];
603     // Convert mode string to enum
604     if (strcmp(mode_str, "server") == 0) {
605       detected_mode = MODE_SERVER;
606     } else if (strcmp(mode_str, "client") == 0) {
607       detected_mode = MODE_CLIENT;
608     } else if (strcmp(mode_str, "mirror") == 0) {
609       detected_mode = MODE_MIRROR;
610     } else if (strcmp(mode_str, "acds") == 0 || strcmp(mode_str, "discovery-service") == 0) {
611       detected_mode = MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE;
612     } else if (strcmp(mode_str, "discovery") == 0) {
613       detected_mode = MODE_DISCOVERY;
614     }
615   }
616   generate_default_log_path(detected_mode, default_log_path, sizeof(default_log_path));
617 
618   const char *log_file = default_log_path;
619   int max_search = (mode_position > 0) ? mode_position : argc;
620   for (int i = 1; i < max_search - 1; i++) {
621     if ((strcmp(argv[i], "--log-file") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "-L") == 0)) {
622       log_file = argv[i + 1];
623       break;
624     }
625   }
626 
627   // EARLY PARSE: Check for --json (JSON logging format)
628   // If JSON format is enabled, we'll use the JSON filename during early init
629   // --json MUST appear BEFORE the mode
630   bool early_json_format = false;
631   if (mode_position > 0) {
632     for (int i = 1; i < mode_position; i++) {
633       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--json") == 0) {
634         early_json_format = true;
635         break;
636       }
637     }
638   } else {
639     // If no mode found, search entire argv
640     for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
641       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--json") == 0) {
642         early_json_format = true;
643         break;
644       }
645     }
646   }
647 
648   // EARLY PARSE: Extract log template from argv (--log-template)
649   // Note: This is for format templates, different from --log-format which is the output format
650   // Binary-level options must appear BEFORE the mode
651   const char *early_log_template = NULL;
652   bool early_log_template_console_only = false;
653   for (int i = 1; i < max_search - 1; i++) {
654     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--log-template") == 0) {
655       early_log_template = argv[i + 1];
656       break;
657     }
658   }
659   for (int i = 1; i < max_search; i++) {
660     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--log-format-console-only") == 0) {
661       early_log_template_console_only = true;
662       break;
663     }
664   }
665 
666   // Initialize shared subsystems BEFORE options_init()
667   // This ensures options parsing can use properly configured logging with colors
668   // If JSON format is requested, don't write text logs to file
669   // All logs will be JSON formatted later once options_init() runs
670   const char *early_log_file = early_json_format ? NULL : log_file;
671   asciichat_error_t init_result = asciichat_shared_init(early_log_file, is_client_like_mode);
672   if (init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
673     return init_result;
674   }
675 
676   // Route logs to stderr if stdout is piped (MUST happen early, before options_init logs)
677   // This keeps stdout clean for data output (e.g., --snapshot mode piped to file)
678   if (terminal_should_force_stderr()) {
679     log_set_force_stderr(true);
680   }
681 
682   // Register cleanup of shared subsystems to run on normal exit
683   // Library code doesn't call atexit() - the application is responsible
684   // IMPORTANT: atexit handlers run in LIFO order - last registered runs first
685   // So register order (top to bottom) = execution order (bottom to top):
686   // 1. PCRE2 cleanup (runs last) - frees compiled regexes after all code stops using them
687   // 2. Path cleanup (runs 3rd) - now a no-op but kept for API compatibility
688   // 3. Shared destroy (runs 2nd) - frees all shared resources
689   // 4. Cursor show (runs 1st) - shows cursor before exit
690   (void)atexit(asciichat_pcre2_cleanup_all);
691   (void)atexit(path_cleanup_thread_locals);
692   (void)atexit(asciichat_shared_destroy);
693   (void)atexit(on_exit_show_cursor);
694 
695   // SECRET: Check for --backtrace (debug builds only) BEFORE options_init()
696   // Prints a backtrace and exits immediately - useful for debugging hangs
697 #ifndef NDEBUG
698   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
699     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--backtrace") == 0) {
700       log_info("=== Backtrace at startup ===");
701       backtrace_print_simple(0);
702       log_info("=== End Backtrace ===");
703       asciichat_shared_destroy();
704       return 0;
705     }
706   }
707 #endif
708 
709   // NOW parse all options - can use logging with colors!
710   asciichat_error_t options_result = options_init(argc, argv);
711   if (options_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
712     asciichat_error_context_t error_ctx;
713     if (HAS_ERRNO(&error_ctx)) {
714       fprintf(stderr, "Error: %s\n", error_ctx.context_message);
715     } else {
716       fprintf(stderr, "Error: Failed to initialize options (error code: %d)\n", options_result);
717     }
718     (void)fflush(stderr);
719 
720     // Clean up options state before exiting
721     options_state_destroy();
722     options_cleanup_schema();
723 
724     exit(options_result);
725   }
726 
727   // Get parsed options
728   const options_t *opts = options_get();
729   if (!opts) {
730     fprintf(stderr, "Error: Options not initialized\n");
731     return 1;
732   }
733 
734   // Determine final log file path (use mode-specific default from options if available)
735   // Determine output format early to decide on filename and logging strategy
736   bool use_json_logging = GET_OPTION(json);
737 
738   // Determine the log filename
739   // Check if user explicitly passed --log-file (not just the mode-specific default from options_init)
740   bool user_specified_log_file = false;
741   for (int i = 1; i < argc - 1; i++) {
742     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--log-file") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "-L") == 0) {
743       user_specified_log_file = true;
744       break;
745     }
746   }
747 
748   const char *final_log_file = opts->log_file;
749   char json_filename_buf[256];
750 
751   if (use_json_logging) {
752     // When JSON logging is enabled, determine JSON output filename
753     if (user_specified_log_file) {
754       // User explicitly specified a log file - use it exactly for JSON output
755       SAFE_STRNCPY(json_filename_buf, final_log_file, sizeof(json_filename_buf) - 1);
756     } else {
757       // Using default: replace .log with .json in the mode-specific default
758       // e.g., server.log -> server.json, mirror.log -> mirror.json, etc.
759       size_t len = strlen(final_log_file);
760       if (len > 4 && strcmp(&final_log_file[len - 4], ".log") == 0) {
761         // File ends with .log - replace with .json
762         SAFE_STRNCPY(json_filename_buf, final_log_file, sizeof(json_filename_buf) - 1);
763         // Replace .log with .json
764         strcpy(&json_filename_buf[len - 4], ".json");
765       } else {
766         // File doesn't end with .log - just append .json
767         SAFE_STRNCPY(json_filename_buf, final_log_file, sizeof(json_filename_buf) - 1);
768         strncat(json_filename_buf, ".json", sizeof(json_filename_buf) - strlen(json_filename_buf) - 1);
769       }
770     }
771     final_log_file = json_filename_buf;
772     // For JSON mode: Initialize logging without file (text will be disabled)
773     // We'll set up JSON output separately below
774     // Note: log_init() was already called in asciichat_shared_init()
775   } else {
776     // Text logging mode: use the file from options (which is mode-specific default or user-specified)
777     // Note: log_init() was already called in asciichat_shared_init()
778   }
779 
780   // Apply custom log template if specified (use early parsed value if available, otherwise use options)
781   const char *final_format = early_log_template ? early_log_template : GET_OPTION(log_template);
782   bool final_format_console_only =
783       early_log_template ? early_log_template_console_only : GET_OPTION(log_format_console_only);
784   if (final_format && final_format[0] != '\0') {
785     asciichat_error_t fmt_result = log_set_format(final_format, final_format_console_only);
786     if (fmt_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
787       log_error("Failed to apply custom log format");
788     }
789   }
790 
791   // Configure JSON output if requested
792   if (use_json_logging) {
793     // Open the JSON file for output
794     int json_fd = platform_open("main_log_file", final_log_file, O_CREAT | O_RDWR | O_TRUNC, FILE_PERM_PRIVATE);
795     if (json_fd >= 0) {
796       log_set_json_output(json_fd);
797     } else {
798       // Failed to open JSON file - report error and exit
799       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to open JSON output file: %s", final_log_file);
800       return ERROR_CONFIG;
801     }
802   }
803 
804   // Initialize colors now that logging is fully initialized
805   // This must happen after log_init() since log_init_colors() checks if g_log.initialized
806   log_init_colors();
807 
808   // Apply quiet mode - disables terminal output
809   // Status screen mode only disables terminal output if terminal is interactive
810   // In non-interactive mode (piped output), logs go to stdout/stderr normally
811   if (GET_OPTION(quiet) ||
812       (opts->detected_mode == MODE_SERVER && GET_OPTION(status_screen) && terminal_is_interactive())) {
813     log_set_terminal_output(false);
814   }
815 
816   // Initialize palette based on command line options
817   const char *custom_chars = opts && opts->palette_custom_set ? opts->palette_custom : NULL;
818   if (apply_palette_config(GET_OPTION(palette_type), custom_chars) != 0) {
819     FATAL(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to apply palette configuration");
820   }
821 
822   // Set quiet mode for memory debugging
823 #if defined(DEBUG_MEMORY) && !defined(USE_MIMALLOC_DEBUG) && !defined(NDEBUG)
824   debug_memory_set_quiet_mode(GET_OPTION(quiet));
825 #endif
826 
827   // Truncate log if it's already too large
828   log_truncate_if_large();
829 
830   // Handle --help and --version (these are detected and flagged by options_init)
831   // Terminal capabilities already initialized before options_init() at startup
832   if (opts->help) {
833     print_usage(opts->detected_mode);
834     fflush(NULL);
835     _Exit(0);
836   }
837 
838   if (opts->version) {
839     log_set_terminal_output(true);
840     action_show_version();
841     // action_show_version() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
842   }
843 
844 #ifndef NDEBUG
845   // Handle --debug-state (debug builds only)
846   // Sleep for specified time AFTER mode initialization so locks are created
847   if (IS_OPTION_EXPLICIT(debug_sync_state_time, opts) && opts->debug_sync_state_time > 0.0) {
848     log_info("Will print sync state after %f seconds", opts->debug_sync_state_time);
849   }
850 #endif
851 
852   // For server mode with status screen: disable terminal output only if interactive
853   // In non-interactive mode (piped output), logs go to stdout/stderr normally
854   // The status screen (when shown) will capture and display logs in its buffer instead
855   if (opts->detected_mode == MODE_SERVER && opts->status_screen && terminal_is_interactive()) {
856     log_set_terminal_output(false);
857   }
858 
859   log_dev("Logging initialized to %s", final_log_file);
860 
861   // Note: We do NOT auto-disable colors when stdout appears to be piped, because:
862   // 1. Tools like ripgrep can display ANSI colors when piped
863   // 2. Sandboxed/containerized environments may report false positives for isatty()
864   // 3. Users can explicitly disable colors with --color=false if needed
865   // Color behavior is now fully controlled by --color and --color-mode options.
866 
867 #ifndef NDEBUG
868   // Initialize lock debugging system after logging is fully set up
869   log_debug("Initializing lock debug system...");
870   int debug_sync_result = debug_sync_init();
871   if (debug_sync_result != 0) {
872     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Debug sync system initialization failed");
873     FATAL(ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, "Debug sync system initialization failed");
874   }
875   log_debug("Debug sync system initialized successfully");
876 
877   // Initialize memory debug system
878   // TEMPORARILY DISABLED: these calls hang in non-interactive mode
879   // log_debug("Initializing memory debug system...");
880   // int debug_memory_result = debug_memory_thread_init();
881   // if (debug_memory_result != 0) {
882   //   LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Memory debug system initialization failed");
883   //   FATAL(ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, "Memory debug system initialization failed");
884   // }
885   // log_debug("Memory debug system initialized successfully");
886 
887   // Start memory debug (now just prints report directly)
888   // if (debug_memory_thread_start() != 0) {
889   //   LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Memory debug startup failed");
890   //   FATAL(ERROR_THREAD, "Memory debug startup failed");
891   // }
892 
893 #ifndef _WIN32
894   // Unblock SIGUSR1 and SIGUSR2 at process level to ensure delivery
895   sigset_t set;
896   sigemptyset(&set);
897   sigaddset(&set, SIGUSR1);
898   sigaddset(&set, SIGUSR2);
899   sigprocmask(SIG_UNBLOCK, &set, NULL);
900 
901   // Register SIGUSR1 to trigger synchronization debugging output in all modes
902   signal_handler_t old_usr1 = platform_signal(SIGUSR1, common_handle_sigusr1);
903   if (old_usr1 == SIG_ERR) {
904     log_warn("Failed to register SIGUSR1 handler");
905   } else {
906     log_debug("SIGUSR1 handler registered successfully");
907   }
908 
909   // Register SIGUSR2 to trigger memory report in all modes
910   signal_handler_t old_usr2 = platform_signal(SIGUSR2, common_handle_sigusr2);
911   if (old_usr2 == SIG_ERR) {
912     log_warn("Failed to register SIGUSR2 handler");
913   } else {
914     log_debug("SIGUSR2 handler registered successfully");
915   }
916 #endif
917 #endif
918 
919   if (opts->fps > 0) {
920     if (opts->fps < 1 || opts->fps > 144) {
921       log_warn("FPS value %d out of range (1-144), using default", opts->fps);
922     } else {
923       log_debug("FPS set from command line: %d", opts->fps);
924     }
925   }
926 
927   // Automatic update check at startup (once per week maximum)
928   if (!GET_OPTION(no_check_update)) {
929     update_check_result_t update_result;
930     asciichat_error_t update_err = update_check_startup(&update_result);
931     if (update_err == ASCIICHAT_OK && update_result.update_available) {
932       char notification[1024];
933       update_check_format_notification(&update_result, notification, sizeof(notification));
934       log_info("%s", notification);
935 
936       // Set update notification for splash/status screens
937       splash_set_update_notification(notification);
938     }
939   }
940 
941   // Set up global signal handlers BEFORE mode dispatch
942   // All modes use the same centralized exit mechanism
943   setup_signal_handlers();
944 
945   // Register application callbacks so lib code can check exit flags
946   // This connects the render loop's should_exit check to signal_exit()
947   static const app_callbacks_t app_callbacks = {
948       .should_exit = should_exit,
949       .signal_exit = signal_exit,
950   };
951   app_callbacks_register(&app_callbacks);
952 
953   // Register shutdown callback so splash thread and other code can check for exit
954   shutdown_register_callback((shutdown_check_fn)should_exit);
955 
956 #ifndef NDEBUG
957   // Start debug threads now, after initialization but before mode entry
958   // This avoids lock contention during critical initialization phase
959   // Note: debug_sync_start_thread() and debug_memory_thread_start() now use
960   // direct pthread calls instead of mutex_init/cond_init to avoid named registry
961   // deadlock during thread startup
962   if (debug_sync_start_thread() != 0) {
963     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Debug sync thread startup failed");
964     FATAL(ERROR_THREAD, "Debug sync thread startup failed");
965   }
966   log_debug("Debug sync thread started");
967 
968 #endif
969 
970   // Find and dispatch to mode entry point
971   const mode_descriptor_t *mode = find_mode(opts->detected_mode);
972   if (!mode) {
973     fprintf(stderr, "Error: Mode not found for detected_mode=%d\n", opts->detected_mode);
974     return 1;
975   }
976 
977   // Call the mode-specific entry point
978   // Mode entry points use options_get() to access parsed options
979   int exit_code = mode->entry_point();
980 
981   // Named registry cleanup is handled by asciichat_shared_destroy()
982   // (called via atexit handler), which runs AFTER the memory report
983 
984   if (exit_code == ERROR_USAGE) {
985     exit(ERROR_USAGE);
986   }
987 
988   return exit_code;
989 }






References action_show_version(),
    app_callbacks_register(), apply_palette_config(),
    asciichat_pcre2_cleanup_all(), asciichat_shared_destroy(),
    asciichat_shared_init(), asciichat_thread_self(),
    backtrace_print_simple(), colorscheme_load_builtin(),
    debug_atomic_init(), debug_memory_set_quiet_mode(),
    debug_sync_init(), debug_sync_set_main_thread_id(),
    debug_sync_start_thread(), detect_terminal_capabilities(),
    mode_descriptor_t::entry_point, g_argc, g_argv,
    g_color_flag_passed, g_color_flag_value, grep_init(),
    log_init_colors(), log_redetect_terminal_capabilities(),
    log_set_color_scheme(), log_set_force_stderr(),
    log_set_format(), log_set_json_output(),
    log_set_terminal_output(), log_truncate_if_large(),
    named_init(), named_registry_register_packet_types(),
    options_cleanup_schema(), options_colorscheme_init_early(),
    options_get(), options_init(), options_state_destroy(),
    path_cleanup_thread_locals(), platform_open(),
    REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS, setup_signal_handlers(),
    should_exit(), shutdown_register_callback(),
    signal_exit(), splash_set_update_notification(),
    terminal_cursor_show(), terminal_is_interactive(),
    terminal_should_force_stderr(),
    update_check_format_notification(), and
    update_check_startup().






void
  set_interrupt_callback (void(* cb )(void))


Register a mode-specific interrupt callback




Called by mode-specific code to register a callback that will be
    invoked when signal_exit() is called. Used to shut down network
    sockets so threads blocked in recv() unblock quickly instead of waiting for
    timeouts.




The callback is called synchronously from signal_exit(), so
    it must be async-signal-safe (only atomics, socket_shutdown(), no
    malloc/etc).




Only one callback can be registered at a time. Setting a new
    callback replaces the previous one.




Parameters


cb Function to call on exit signal, or NULL to
  unregister


Definition at line 215 of file main.c.


215                                               {
216   g_interrupt_callback = cb;
217 }






Referenced by client_main(), and
  discovery_main().






void
  setup_signal_handlers (void )


Set up global signal handlers Called once at startup before mode
    dispatch


Definition at line 316 of file main.c.


316                                  {
317   platform_set_console_ctrl_handler(console_ctrl_handler);
318 
319 #ifndef _WIN32
320   platform_signal_handler_t handlers[] = {
321       {SIGINT, handle_sigterm},
322       {SIGTERM, handle_sigterm},
323       {SIGPIPE, SIG_IGN},
324   };
325   platform_register_signal_handlers(handlers, 3);
326   log_debug("Signal handlers registered");
327 #endif
328 }






Referenced by main().






bool should_exit (void )


Check if the application should exit




Called by mode main loops to detect shutdown requests from signals
    or errors.




Returns


true if shutdown has been requested, false
  otherwise


Definition at line 199 of file main.c.


199                        {
200   return atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit);
201 }






References atomic_load_bool(), and
  g_should_exit.


Referenced by client_main(),
    connection_attempt_tcp(), connection_attempt_websocket(),
    display_full_reset(), main(),
    server_connection_establish(), session_client_like_run(),
    session_pipeline_run_main(), and session_render_loop().






void
  signal_exit (void )


Signal that the application should exit




Sets the global exit flag and calls the registered interrupt
    callback if set. Safe to call from signal handlers (uses only atomics and
    function pointers). Called by signal handlers (SIGTERM, Ctrl+C) and normal
    code (errors, timeouts).


Definition at line 203 of file main.c.


203                        {
204   // Note: This function may be called from a signal handler context where
205   // other threads may hold mutex locks. We avoid log_console() here to prevent
206   // deadlock (render loop holds terminal lock, signal handler can't acquire it).
207   // The shutdown will be logged by normal thread context later.
208   atomic_store_bool(&g_should_exit, true);
209   void (*cb)(void) = g_interrupt_callback;
210   if (cb) {
211     cb();
212   }
213 }






References atomic_store_bool(), and
  g_should_exit.


Referenced by client_main(), main(), and
    session_render_loop().
